handoff.reason: Scoping complete: phased_implementation plan with 4 phases + 14 ACs + 5 decision records (DR-BRAND-A..E). No stub predecessor — scoped directly per bulletproof series pattern. Path inventory (Phase 1) is non-skippable: the exact string parameters that require branding, and their brand classification (absolute/relative/either), are confirmed only by reading the source at execution time. No ORCHESTRATOR ruling required before Phase 1 begins. Standing by for ORCHESTRATOR to dispatch CODEREF-CORE for Phase 1 execution.

execution_rules.phase_gates: Phase 1 (path inventory) gates Phase 2 (path-types.ts definition); Phase 2 gates Phase 3 (propagation through chunk-converter.ts + indexing-orchestrator.ts); Phase 3 gates Phase 4 (test updates + closeout-ready). Each phase requires tsc clean both configs + all rag-suite + pipeline tests green.
execution_rules.atomic_commits: Phase 1: 1 (inventory doc); Phase 2: 1 (path-types.ts); Phase 3: 2-4 (per-file propagation, one commit per file or logical grouping); Phase 4: 1 (test updates + closeout). Total 5-7 anticipated.
execution_rules.tsc_invariant: tsc --noEmit clean both configs (tsconfig.json + tsconfig.cli.json) after each commit
execution_rules.frozen_fixture_invariant: Per Phase 3/4 commit, vitest run against frozen-fixture dual-AC test must PASS
execution_rules.real_world_substrate_identity: AC-05a 2415===2415 + AC-05b 263===263 must PASS at close (verified via predecessor WO static probe verify-dual-ac.mjs)

hard_constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened

Predecessor archives untouched (rag-index-seam-deletion, rag-index-frozen-fixture-dual-ac-invariants, rag-index-single-analyzer-slice, cleanup-indexing-orchestrator-analyzerservice-param, path-normalization-fix, pipeline-indexing-rag)
Boundary: src/integration/rag/*.ts ONLY — no changes to pipeline/, scanner/, analyzer/, or any layer outside src/integration/rag/
Frozen-fixture file contents (src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/*) are FROZEN — do NOT modify
Frozen-fixture CI-blocking invariant must PASS at every Phase 3/4 commit
AC-05a real-world 2415===2415 + AC-05b real-world 263===263 preserved at close
Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT block (lines 32-70 of __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts) untouched
path-types.ts must NOT introduce runtime overhead beyond a brand cast — no actual filesystem checks, no path resolution side-effects in the branded type constructors
path.join(this.basePath, filePath) call sites STAY — branding makes their inputs typed, not removed
Any branding propagation required outside src/integration/rag/ → HALT (boundary violation)
tsc --noEmit clean both configs after each commit
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode PASS clean (acknowledge pre-existing stubs.json schema drift per STUB-044)

blockers.description: ORCHESTRATOR ruling required on DR-BRAND-D (boundary violation). src/cli/rag-index.ts:439 constructs new IndexingOrchestrator(..., args.projectDir) — outside src/integration/rag/ boundary. Option A: extend boundary (1 file/1 line change in src/cli/); Option B: toAbsolute() cast inside IndexingOrchestrator constructor (keeps CLI unbranded); Option C: scope to ChunkConverter only (leaves orchestrator/incremental-indexer unbranded). Phase 3 T3.1 (ChunkConverter propagation) is unblocked. T3.2 (IndexingOrchestrator propagation) + IncrementalIndexer propagation are blocked until ruling.

phase_transitions.label: Path inventory
phase_transitions.notes: HALT-BOUNDARY-VIOLATION surfaced: src/cli/rag-index.ts constructs new IndexingOrchestrator(..., args.projectDir). Three options (A/B/C) raised for ORCHESTRATOR ruling per DR-BRAND-D before Phase 3 propagation into IndexingOrchestrator. ChunkConverter propagation (Phase 3 T3.1) is unblocked — all its call sites are within src/integration/rag/. IncrementalIndexer propagation is contingent on orchestrator ruling (all its construction passes this.basePath from IndexingOrchestrator).

communication_log.summary: Phase 1 inventory complete (inventory.md authored). tsc PASS both configs. HALT-BOUNDARY-VIOLATION: src/cli/rag-index.ts:439 constructs IndexingOrchestrator with args.projectDir (outside src/integration/rag/ boundary). BLOCK-BRAND-BOUNDARY raised. Three options for ORCHESTRATOR ruling: (A) extend boundary — src/cli/ in scope, toAbsolute() at CLI call site; (B) internal cast — constructor accepts string, casts to AbsolutePath internally; (C) scope to ChunkConverter only. ChunkConverter Phase 3 T3.1 is unblocked (all call sites within boundary). Proceeding to Phase 2 (path-types.ts) and Phase 3 T3.1 while awaiting ruling on T3.2+.

WO-RAG-INDEX-BRANDED-PATHS-001 authored at daily-agent-session-2026-05-08 (bulletproof concept #4 of 6). Scoped directly — no predecessor stub. Plan_type=phased_implementation; 4 phases (inventory → path-types.ts → propagation → closeout); 5-7 atomic commits; 14 ACs; 5 decision records (DR-BRAND-A..E). Predecessor WO-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 (CORE cfd485a) is the immediate predecessor; frozen-fixture invariant (CORE 282382a) is the live safety net. Boundary strictly src/integration/rag/*.ts — no ruling required before Phase 1; Phase 1 inventory is non-skippable and determines exact brand classifications (absolute/relative/either) per path parameter. path.join() call sites stay; branding makes inputs typed. Standing by for ORCHESTRATOR to dispatch CODEREF-CORE for Phase 1 execution.

decisions_log.rationale: Scoped directly per bulletproof series pattern (no stub predecessor). Plan_type=phased_implementation: Phase 1 inventory is non-skippable because exact per-parameter brand classification (absolute/relative/either) requires reading every path-bearing method signature in src/integration/rag/*.ts at execution time. DR-BRAND-A..E recorded covering plan_type, boundary enforcement, path-types.ts design, propagation strategy, and halt conditions.
